description: Focuses on creating accessible technology and instructional media, developing long-term resources, advancing accessible social interaction between students and students with instructors, and using case studies as examples of good practices.
prerequisites: DRB 601 (or concurrent), DRB 613/DRB 614 (or concurrent); or consent.

subject: DRB
title: DRB Research Rotation
description: Introductory laboratory research experience under the supervision of faculty.

subject: DRB
title: Directed Research
description: Research experience in developmental and reproductive biology.

subject: DRB
title: Dissertation Research
description: Disability and Diversity Studies

subject: DIS
title: Foundations in Disability and Diversity
description: DIS 606 - Introduction to Research in Curriculum and Teaching - Credits: 3
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Summer only

subject: DIS
title: Sexuality & Health
description: responsibility for health.

subject: DIS
title: Supporting Multilingual Learners
description: Strategies for identifying and adopting best practices that support all students in inclusive learning environments with an emphasis on STEM curriculum for English Language Learners; (B) science; (C) technology; (D) math.

subject: DIS
title: Practicum in Applied Behavior Analysis
description: DIS 683 - Interdisciplinary Disability and Diversity Issues - Credits: 3 Develop understanding of current issues faced by individuals with disabilities and their families within the disability paradigm and across cultures.

subject: DIS
title: Interdisciplinary Team Development
description: Supervised field experience in applied behavior analysis. The format is a combination of fieldwork and seminar meetings. Assessment methods and techniques to instructional research-based practices and supports across disciplines, cultures and PK-12 that address barriers to learning for all students with the context of the Comprehensive Student Support System (CSSS).

subject: DIS
title: Disability History Through the Ages
description: A historical overview of disability that visits diverse perspectives, attitudes, and treatment toward individuals with disabilities through many centuries, societies, and cultures throughout the world; and how people with disabilities perceived themselves.

subject: DIS
title: Multicultural Issues in Disabilities
description: Provide background and understanding of various Pacific Rim cultural issues in the conceptualization and treatment of individuals with disabilities and their families.

subject: DIS
title: Advanced Interdisciplinary Assessment
description: Assessment methods and techniques to instructional research-based practices and supports across disciplines, cultures and PK-12 that address barriers to learning for all students with the context of the Comprehensive Student Support System (CSSS).

subject: DIS
title: Seminar on Disability Issues
description: In-depth explorations of current and compelling interdisciplinary topics related to advancing the social, political, educational, and economic integration of individuals with disabilities of all ages.

subject: DIS
title: Portfolio/Field Work
description: Interdisciplinary community experience and portfolio in disability and diversity studies.

subject: EPET
title: Introduction to Human Spaceflight
description: Effects of spaceflight conditions on human physiology and psychology. Astronaut selection, training, crew composition. Ethics of space exploration. Politics & sociology of public and private human space flight. Selection of capstone project. Significant written communication.

subject: DIS
title: Mixed Methods Research Design
description: Mixed methods research is designed for PhD and masters students in education and social sciences considering combining qualitative and quantitative research. Covers philosophical and practical implications culminating in a mixed methods dissertation/thesis proposal. Earth and Planetary Exploration Technology
repeatable: 1 time Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year

subject: EPET
title: Space Exploration
description: Introduction to science and engineering of Solar System exploration with laboratory exercises. Covers planetary science, model-based systems engineering, mission planning, constraints on payload and spacecraft design, human spaceflight technology. Research projects emphasize written communication.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: EPET
title: Space Science and Instrumentation
description: Essential techniques for remote compositional analysis of planets; understanding spectroscopy, mineralogy, and geochemistry of planetary surfaces and their measurement. Design of space flight instrumentation. Significant oral and written communication is required.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only.

subject: EPET
title: Space Mission Design
description: Covers all aspects of spacecraft design, subsystems, science payload, systems engineering, project management, budgets, and ethical issues that are important to consider in producing a fully successful mission.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: EPET
title: Human Spaceflight Mission Elements
description: Engineering aspects of spacecraft design, subsystems, science payload, systems engineering, project management, and budgets for human spaceflight missions. Design of environmental control and life support systems. Capstone project design. Significant oral and written communication. EPET 421 - Capstone Project - Human Spaceflight Analog Mission - Credits: 4 Science experiments in space and human space flight engineering. Human spaceflight capstone project with a multidisciplinary team of engineers, scientists, and social scientists. Concurrent disciplinary and multidisciplinary methodologies. Human spaceflight analog mission. Significant written communication. Earth Sciences
prerequisites: EPET 420 Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ERTH
title: Geology of the Hawaiian Islands
description: Hawaiian geology and geologic processes: origin of Hawaiian islands, volcanism, rocks and minerals, landforms, stream and coastal processes, landslides, earthquakes and tsunamis, groundwater, geologic and environmental hazards. Field trip.

subject: ERTH
title: Volcanoes in the Sea
description: Lecture with field trips covering the manner in which geological conditions, resources, and events have affected past and present circum-Pacific societies.

subject: ERTH
title: Dynamic Earth
description: The natural physical environment; the landscape; rocks and minerals, rivers and oceans; volcanism, earthquakes, and other processes inside the Earth; effects of human use of the Earth and its resources. Field trip.

subject: ERTH
title: Dynamic Earth Laboratory
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Hands-on study of minerals, rocks, and topographic maps. Examine volcanism, hydrology, coastal processes and hazards, geologic time and earthquakes. Field trips to investigate landslides, beaches and Oʻahu geology.
gen_ed: DY ERTH 102 - Quantifying Global and Environmental Change - Credits: 3 Introductory mathematical approaches to quantifying key aspects of global and environmental change. Includes data analysis, graphical representation and modeling of population growth, greenhouse gas emissions and fate, sustainable resource utilization, and sea level change.

subject: ERTH
title: Voyage through the Solar System
description: An illustrated voyage through the Solar System based on recent scientific results. The class highlights the origin, evolution, and current knowledge of the eight planets, their moons, asteroids, comets, and one star, the Sun. Field trip.

subject: ERTH
title: Humans and the Environment
description: Prepares students to make decisions such as where to build/buy a house, sustainable use of natural resources, and what environmental actions relevant to society and Earth's ecosystem are appropriate on a local and global scale.

subject: ERTH
title: Solar System Studio
description: Explore the wonders of the Solar System through handson experience of science. Will study satellite images, evaluate planetary surface observations, analyze data of objects in our Solar System, conduct experiments, and communicate their findings. ERTH 110 - Voyage of the Vicariance: Earth and Life through Time - Credits: 3

subject: ERTH
title: Introduction to Directed Research
description: Lower division reading and research in any area of ERTH under the direction of a faculty member.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Introduction to Volcanoes
description: Examines the origin and classification of volcanoes, volcanic eruptions, and volcanic deposits. Includes the history of volcanic studies, myths, and legends. Emphasis on volcanic eruptions, eruptive products, volcanic hazards, and risk management. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ERTH
title: Geological Hazards
description: Introductory course covering the causes of, and effects from, earthquakes, tsunami, volcanic eruptions, landslides, rockfalls, and other natural geologic phenomena. Field trips. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Natural Disasters and Human History
description: Examines how natural hazards have affected the course of human culture and societies from pre-history to the present in Hawaiʻi/Oceania, Asia, Africa, Europe, and the Americas.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ERTH
title: Physical Geology
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Structure, composition and evolution of Earth; processes responsible for formation, deformation and transformation of rocks; plate tectonics. Emphasis on quantitative methods, problem solving and critical thinking to geology. Laboratory and field trips required. (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Origin and age of the solar system and earth: interior of the earth; plate tectonics and records of biological evolution and past environments. Development of critical thinking and writing skills.
prerequisites: ERTH 101L and one of ERTH 101, ERTH 103, ERTH 104, ERTH 106, or ERTH 130; or consent. ERTH 101L may be taken concurrently.
major_restrictions: ERTH and GEOL majors only. Consent required for all non-majors.

subject: ERTH
title: Climate Change
description: Introduction to the science of climate change focusing on Earth's past, present and future, specifically, the causes, consequences, and record of past changes; future projections, mitigation, and adaptation. Appropriate for both science and non-science majors.
prerequisites: ERTH 101 or ERTH 103 or ERTH 104 or ERTH 106 or ERTH 111 or ERTH 130 or ATMO 101 or GES 102, or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Scientific Programming
description: (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Introduction to solving scientific problems by computer programming. Overview of the MatLab programming language and environment. Emphasis placed on good style, logical reasoning, and applied mathematics.

subject: ERTH
title: Physical Volcanology
description: Volcanic eruptions and their consequences. Includes models for volcanic eruptions including explosive eruptions and lava flows, monitoring of active volcanoes, evaluation and impacts of volcanic hazards, and mitigation of volcanic risk. Field trips. Normally fall. ERTH and GEOL majors or consent. grade of B or consent. ERTH 309 - Sedimentology Processes and Environments - Credits: 4

subject: ERTH
title: Mineralogy
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Introduction to the chemistry, crystalline structure, geologic occurrence, and physical properties of minerals. Development of critical thinking and writing skills.
prerequisites: ERTH 200 or ERTH 201 and (CHEM 162/CHEM 162L or CHEM 171/CHEM 171L). or consent.

subject: ERTH
title: Igneous Petrology
description: (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Survey of composition, classification, and occurrence of igneous rocks. Hand-specimen identification and optical petrography of igneous rocks. Development of critical thinking and writing skills.

subject: ERTH
title: Natural Hazards & Geomechanics
description: (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Dynamic behavior of Earth's surface, physical processes driving geohazards, and the risks posed to society. Theory, practical techniques, and data visualization to measure, analyze, and interpret hazards and implications.
prerequisites: ERTH 101 or ERTH 103 or ERTH 104 or ERTH 106 or ERTH 130, MATH 215 or MATH 241 or MATH 251A, and PHYS 151 or PHYS 170; or consent.

subject: ERTH
title: Geological Field Methods
description: Methods used in geological investigations in the field. Eight hours on Saturday in the field.
prerequisites: ERTH 333; or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ERTH
title: Work of Water
description: Physical properties of water, geological aspects of surface water and ground water occurrence, surface water and groundwater resources, use, and problems.
gen_ed: DY, DP ERTH 312 - Advanced Mathematics for Scientists and Engineers I - Credits: 3 Advanced mathematical methods with emphasis on application to the earth and ocean sciences and engineering. Topics include linear algebra, vector calculus, ordinary differential equations, and numerical methods.
repeatable: Repeatable one time. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only.

subject: ERTH
title: Plate Tectonics and Geodynamics
description: Mechanics of lithosphere deformation related to plate convergence, rifting and strike-slip faulting, and the evolution of mountain belts. Use of theory and skillbuilding techniques to measure, describe, analyze, and interpret structures on Earth.
prerequisites: ERTH 200 or ERTH 201; and ERTH 250; and MATH 241 or MATH 251A; and PHYS 170.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Physics of Earth and Planets
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Essentials of geophysics: application of physics understanding Earth, planetary, and solar dynamics. Topics include heat flow, gravity, geodesy, electromagnetism, seismology. Applications to core and mantle dynamics, plate tectonics, volcanoes, hydrology, environmental concerns, and archaeology.
prerequisites: ERTH 250, ERTH 303, ERTH 313, MATH 242, and PHYS 272, PHYS 272L; or consent.

subject: ERTH
title: Geochemistry
description: Theory and applications of chemical principles and chemical analysis to Earth, ocean and environmental sciences; chemistry of hydrosphere-geospherebiosphere system, origin/ differentiation of Earth/Solar system, volcanic processes, natural radioactivity, organic/inorganic chemistry.

subject: ERTH
title: Introduction to Mineral Physics
prerequisites: ERTH 302 and PHYS 272, or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Earth Materials and Structures
description: (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Lecture and lab that covers formation, occurrence, and identification of common minerals, rocks, and geologic structures. Lab work will include study of hand-samples, thin-sections, and field experiences.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Undergraduate Internship
description: Experiential approach to earth science; students serve as interns to field professionals; responsibilities include supervised field work. Open to undergraduate SOEST majors.

subject: ERTH
title: Undergraduate Research in Earth Sciences
description: Research experience for Earth Science undergraduates. Under the guidance of a mentor, the student will conduct research and write a report on the background, methods, data, interpretations, and conclusions of the work.

subject: ERTH
title: Directed Reading
description: Directed reading in earth sciences. Scientific study of the materials that make up the Earth. Properties of minerals on micro- and macro-scales; their properties and behavior.

subject: ERTH
title: Hawaiian Geology
prerequisites: ERTH 101, ERTH 103, ERTH 104, or ERTH 170. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year
gen_ed: DP ERTH 404 - Remote Compositional Analysis: Spectroscopy, Mineralogy, and Geochemistry of Planetary Surfaces - Credits: 4 Essential techniques for remote compositional analysis of planets; understanding spectroscopy, mineralogy, and geochemistry of planetary surfaces. Comparative studies of fundamental planetary science phenomena. Planetary surface science discoveries. Sustainability of planetary environments.
repeatable: 1 time ERTH 406 - Natural Disasters: Geoethics and the Layman - Credits: 3 Evaluates ethical practice of geoscience as it relates to studies of natural disasters that result from geological and meteorological phenomena and the means that earth scientists interact with the laymen.

subject: ERTH
title: Energy and Mineral Resources
description: Lecture and discussion on the origin, distribution and exploitation of fossil fuels, renewable energy resources and ore deposits. Coverage and detail will depend partly on student interest and background.

subject: ERTH
title: Environmental Geochemistry
description: Theory and applications of contaminant/pollutant distribution in the hydrosphere-geosphere-biosphereatmosphere system, remediation methods, prevention, industrial/agricultural best practices. Topics include aqueous geochemistry, organic, inorganic, gas phase, and ecosystem impacts of environmental contaminants.
prerequisites: CHEM 161 and CHEM 162, or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ERTH
title: Earth: A Model Planet
description: Integrative course that explores the interactions between the solid earth, oceans, atmosphere, and space environment, and introduces methodology and tools for computer modeling of the Earth as a system and its longterm climate and habitability.
prerequisites: (ERTH 101 and ERTH 250 , and at least one of ERTH 302, ERTH 309, ERTH 313, ERTH 323, ERTH 325, or ERTH 333) with a minimum grade of B.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt. years: spring ERTH 413 - Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis - Credits: 3 Exploratory data analysis, error propagation, probability theory and statistics, curve fitting, regression, sequence and spectral analysis, multivariate analysis, and analysis of directional data.

subject: ERTH
title: Coastal Geochemistry
description: Covers aqueous geochemistry at the land-ocean interface, including the distribution of major and minor ions, trace metals, organic chemicals, and nutrients.
prerequisites: ERTH 200 or ERTH 201 , CHEM 162 , and MATH 241 or MATH 251A ; or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt. years: spring.

subject: ERTH
title: Plate Tectonics
description: (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Quantitative geometrical analysis techniques of plate tectonics theory; instantaneous and finite rotation poles; triple-junction analysis; plate boundary stresses.
prerequisites: ERTH 200 or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Earthquakes and Crustal Deformation
description: Earthquakes and crustal deformation through modern seismological and geodetic observations; elastic properties of rocks, seismic waves, causes, detection, and location of earthquakes; crustal motions of the earthquake cycle; tsunami generation, liquefaction, and planetary observations.
prerequisites: MATH 241 and PHYS 170, or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years
class_standing: Junior standing or higher, or consent.

subject: ERTH
title: Engineering Geology
description: Solutions of geotechnical problems by geologists and engineers through recognition, characterization, evaluation, and assessment of geologic processes that impact people, engineering structures, and engineering operations. Group format.
prerequisites: consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only
major_restrictions: ERTH, GEOL, and CEE majors only.

subject: ERTH
title: Hydrogeology
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Occurrence, characteristics, movement, quality, development, and contamination of water in the Earth's crust.
prerequisites: ERTH 200 or ERTH 201 , and MATH 215 or MATH 241

subject: ERTH
title: Geological Remote Sensing
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Combined lecture-lab on the concepts behind, geologic uses for, and techniques of satellite and airborne remote sensing. Lab work will consist of computer image processing. Field trips. Open to nonmajors.
prerequisites: ERTH 200 or ERTH 201 or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt. years: spring

subject: ERTH
title: Geospatial Information
description: Combined lecture/lab covering the collection, analysis and use of geospatially registered field data.
prerequisites: (ERTH 200 or ERTH 201) or equivalent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Planetary Geology
description: Comparative geology of the terrestrial planets (moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, and Earth); impact cratering, volcanism, tectonism, geomorphology, weathering; manned and unmanned space exploration.

subject: ERTH
title: Lunar Geology
description: Introduction to the basics of the geology and spectroscopy of our Moon.
grade_option: A-F only. Senior standing or higher. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Origin of the Solar System
description: and its constituents (planets, asteroids, stars, comets, and meteorites).
prerequisites: ERTH 101 or ERTH 103 or ERTH 104 or ERTH 106 or ERTH 130 and CHEM 161 and PHYS 170.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt. years: spring

subject: ERTH
title: Undergraduate Thesis
description: Directed research in which the student carries out a scientific project of small to moderate scope with one or more chosen advisors. The student must complete a document in the style of a scientific journal article.

subject: ERTH
title: Earth and Planetary Sciences Workshops
description: Designed for in-service school professionals to learn new approaches and concepts in the fields of earth and planetary sciences.
repeatable: Repeatable for credit. Credits earned in these courses cannot be applied for graduate degrees.

subject: ERTH
title: Equations of Geophysics
description: Least-square approximation of functions by orthogonal series; potential, wave heat flow equations; boundary value problems; Bessel Hankel functions, spherical harmonics, potential theory, plane waves, spherical waves; emphasis on geophysics application.
prerequisites: MATH 244 or MATH 253A, PHYS 400, or consent.

subject: ERTH
title: Explosive Volcanism
description: Explosive volcanic eruptions: from causes to consequences. Review of current physical volcanology including ascent and fragmentation of magma, transport and deposition processes in pyroclastic eruptions, volcanic crisis management and volcanic eruption scenarios. Seven-day field trip.

subject: ERTH
title: Theoretical Petrology
description: Derivation of phase diagrams from basic thermodynamics principles. Equilibria of natural silicate systems. Crystal chemistry, kinetics, diffusion, etc., applied to igneous and metamorphic petrology.
prerequisites: ERTH 302, ERTH 325, and CHEM 351 (or concurrent); or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Petrology of Ocean Lithosphere
description: (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Petrogenesis of the oceanic lithosphere, including mantle processes and rocks from mid-ocean ridges, seamounts, oceanic hotspots, backarc basins, and intra-oceanic arcs.
prerequisites: PLAN 670 or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year

subject: ERTH
title: Lava Flow Rheology and Morphology
description: Effusion eruptions: from eruption to final flow form. Includes: rheology, effusion rate, heat loss, and field measurements, followed by inflation, flow forms, lava lakes, domes, flow hazard and modeling. Field trips to Kilauea and Makapuu.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Current Events in Volcanology
description: Discussion of active areas of volcanism and new publications on volcanology.

subject: ERTH
title: Submarine Volcanoes
description: Seminar exploring different aspects of submarine effusive and explosive volcanism, hydrothermal activity, and volcano-hosted ecosystems.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt. years: fall

subject: ERTH
title: Isotopes and Trace Elements
description: Principles of radiogenic and stable isotope, and trace element geochemistry as applied to igneous petrology, mantle dynamics, plate tectonics, and terrestrial evolution.
prerequisites: ERTH 302 and ERTH 325. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Graduate Teaching Geology
description: For ERTH graduate students who lead, under faculty supervision, a scheduled class in Geology and Geophysics. The instructor will define the student's responsibilities when offering ERTH 609, and these responsibilities must be met for a passing grade. As the consent to take the class must also be granted by the department chair, the chair will also review the responsibilities required by the instructor.
prerequisites: consent of instructor and department chair.

subject: ERTH
title: Graduate Seminar
description: Seminar in which students present a 15- to 20-minute talk on their research or a related topic. Meets once a week with two to three talks per meeting. Graduate students are required to register for this course once per year. or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only
repeatable: 8 times ERTH 611 - Accelerated Introduction to Earth Sciences I - Credits: 3 Lecture presenting a rapid-paced survey of earth sciences for graduate students. Includes origin of the Solar System, tectonics, volcanology, whole-earth composition, phase transformations, petrology (igneous, metamorphic, and sedimentary), historical geology, and hydrology. Saturday field trips. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only ERTH 613 - Introduction to Statistics and Data Analysis - Credits: 3 Exploratory data analysis, error propagation, probability theory and statistics, curve fitting, regression, sequence and spectral analysis, multivariate analysis, and analysis of directional data. Credit earned only one time for either ERTH 413 or 613.

subject: ERTH
title: Electron Microprobe Analysis
description: Combined lecture-lab on the principles of geochemical analysis by electron microprobe and X-ray fluorescence. Hands-on experience with the electron microprobe. Required to operate the UH electron microprobe.

subject: ERTH
title: Literate Programming With R and RStudio
description: Literate programming with R, RStudio, and R Markdown for data analysis and research. Introduction to Matlab for rapid modeling. Emphasis on the preparation of research papers for scholarly publication in the Earth and environmental sciences.
prerequisites: competence in basic differential and integral calculus. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: How to Write a Scientific Paper
description: Designed for students who have collected data and want to know how to publish their work in a scientific journal. Covers the essential parts of paper preparation and submission.
prerequisites: consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Summer only
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt. years: fall
major_restrictions: ERTH students only. ERTH 617 - Summer Fieldschool Program: Hydrogeophysics in Volcanic Environments - Credits: V Will cover the full hydrogeophysical workflow including theory, acquisition design, field data acquisition, data processing, data inversion, and hydrogeological interpretation. Methods include ambient seismic, 3D electrical resistivity tomography and induced polarization, and self-potential.

subject: ERTH
title: Coastal Geology
description: Geological history and geologic framework of the Hawaiian shoreline. Modern climate change, paleoclimate, focus on sea level change. Modern coastal management and problems in the coastal environment. Coastal planning.

subject: ERTH
title: Advanced Environmental Geochemistry
description: Theory and applications of contaminant/pollutant distribution in the hydrosphere-geosphere-biosphereatmosphere system and remediation methods. Topics include aqueous geochemistry, organic, inorganic, gas phase and isotope chemistry of environmental contaminants.
prerequisites: ERTH 325 or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ERTH
title: Numerical Modeling of Physical Systems
description: Finite difference, finite element, and other modeling techniques applied to geological and geophysical problems. Physical modeling of heat flow, molecular diffusion, solidification and melting, deformation, fluid flow, wave propagation, and other phenomena. mantle convection is a major control on Earth's internal structure.
prerequisites: (with a minimum grade of B-) for PHYS 170, PHYS 272, and MATH 307 or ERTH 312 (or equivalent). Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year ERTH 631 - Geophysics-Solid, Fluid, and Wave Mechanics - Credits: 3 Continuum mechanics in geophysics, as applied to the deformation of Earth materials (elastic, viscous, viscoelastic, and plastic deformations) and seismic wave propagation (body waves, surface waves, anisotropy, and attenuation).

subject: ERTH
title: Seismology
description: Elasticity, wave equations, body waves, surface waves, free oscillations, seismometry, seismogram interpretation, tectonics, inversion, source theory, and waveform modeling.

subject: ERTH
title: Earth System Science and Global Change
description: Global view of the planet and how it functions as an integrated unit. Biogeochemical processes, dynamics, and cycles, and analysis of natural and human-induced environmental change. Chemical history of oceanatmospheric-sediment system and coevolution of the biota.
prerequisites: BS in environmentally related science or one year of chemistry, physics, and calculus; or consent.

subject: ERTH
title: Stable Isotope Biogeochemistry
description: Stable isotope geochemistry applied to questions of biogeochemical cycling in the oceans, sediment diagenesis, paleoceanography, environmental geochemistry and ecology.
prerequisites: ERTH 325 or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Coastal Geochemistry
description: subsequent diagenesis of modern and ancient sediments. Petrogenesis of siliciclastic, carbonate and orthochemical rocks. Sedimentology, sedimentary petrography and geochemistry.
repeatable: 1 time Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Elemental Composition Changes
description: Changes in the chemical composition of meteorites, bulk Earth, Earth's mantle and crust, sedimentary rocks, hydrosphere and biosphere, and underlying principles.
prerequisites: consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Sedimentary Geochemistry
description: Geochemical thermodynamics and kinetics and their use in interpreting the origin of sediments, sedimentary rocks, and natural waters over a range of pressuretemperature conditions.
prerequisites: CHEM 171, or CHEM 161 and CHEM 162; PHYS 152; and MATH 242 or MATH 252A; and consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Geomagnetism and Cosmic Magnetism
description: Magnetic fields of Earth, planets, stars, and galaxies; dynamo theories; paleomagnetism; terrestrial and lunar rock magnetism; planetary, regional, and local geomagnetic sounding.
prerequisites: consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Groundwater Contamination
description: Principles of groundwater chemistry; chemical evolution in natural groundwater flow systems; sources of contamination; mass transport processes; hydrochemical behavior of contaminants.

subject: ERTH
title: Origin of Sedimentary Rocks
description: Introduction to the finite-difference method; steadystate and transient groundwater flow in saturated and unsaturated media; applications to groundwater (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Environment of deposition and

subject: ERTH
title: Groundwater Modeling
description: recharge and aquifer evaluation.

subject: ERTH
title: Remote Sensing: volcanoes
description: Spectroscopic, radar, thermal, and other methods for remote sensing applied to geologic problems; instrumental design and data analysis. (B) planets; (C) volcanoes.

subject: ERTH
title: Astrochemistry-A Molecular Approach
description: Formation of astrobiologically important molecules and their precursors in the interstellar medium and in our solar system: first principles and latest trends.
prerequisites: consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Fall only

subject: ERTH
title: Planetary Surfaces
description: Comparative geology of terrestrial planets (moon, Mars, Mercury, Venus, and Earth); impact cratering, volcanism, geomorphology; remote sensing; manned and unmanned space exploration.
prerequisites: ERTH 601, ASTR 630; or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: Cosmochemistry
description: Formation and evolution of planets as astrophysical objects, geologic bodies, and abodes of life; current understanding from studies of the Solar System, star formation, meteorites, exoplanets; theory of formation and dynamics; atmospheres, oceans, habitability, biosignatures.
prerequisites: ERTH 325 or CHEM 351 (or equivalent); or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: (Alpha) Remote Sensing
description: Spectroscopic, radar, thermal, and other methods for remote sensing applied to geologic problems; instrumental design and data analysis. (B) planets; (C) volcanoes.

subject: ERTH
title: Remote Sensing: planets
description: Spectroscopic, radar, thermal, and other methods for remote sensing applied to geologic problems; instrumental design and data analysis. (B) planets; (C) volcanoes.

subject: ERTH
title: Seminar in Tectonics
description: Evolution of ocean basins, margins, foldbelts, and platforms, from plate tectonics and regional syntheses of structure, petrology, geophysics, and stratigraphy.
repeatable: 8 times Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years

subject: ERTH
title: (Alpha) Extraterrestrial Material
description: Mineralogical and compositional characteristics of extraterrestrial matter and the implications for the origin and history of the solar system. The subject is treated in two full-semester courses: (B) meteorites; (C) petrology of the Moon and Mars.

subject: ERTH
title: Extraterrestrial Material: meteorites
description: Mineralogical and compositional characteristics of extraterrestrial matter and the implications for the origin and history of the solar system. The subject is treated in two full-semester courses: (B) meteorites; (C) petrology of the Moon and Mars.

subject: ERTH
title: Paleoceanography

Additional topics include the effects of international rate on the welfare of a nation and the effects of different competitive market structures on society.

subject: ECON

description: An introduction to macroeconomics-the study of the overall economy. Topics include the determination of national income, causes and effects of inflation, unemployment, and income inequality; causes and consequences of international differences in economic growth; sources of business cycle expansions and contractions; role of government policy in stabilizing the economy and promoting long-term growth; financial markets and monetary policy; taxes, spending, consequences of budget deficits, determination of trade imbalances, exchange rate fluctuations, and balance of payment crises.

subject: ECON
title: Principles of Macroeconomics
description: An introduction to macroeconomics-the study of the overall economy. Topics include the determination of national income, causes and effects of inflation, unemployment, and income inequality; causes and consequences of international differences in economic growth; sources of business cycle expansions and contractions; role of government policy in stabilizing the economy and promoting long-term growth; financial markets and monetary policy; taxes, spending, consequences of budget deficits, determination of trade imbalances, exchange rate fluctuations, and balance of payment crises.

subject: ECON

subject: ECON
title: Intermediate Macroeconomics
description: and long-run growth; causes and consequences of unemployment, inflation, and business cycle fluctuations; determination of foreign exchange rates and current account imbalances, and the role of government policy in various settings.

Learn to use economic reasoning to understand the social consequences of decisions made by individual consumers, producers, and governments. Analyze the nature of market outcomes under alternative market structures, and further discuss possible welfare-improving government policies when markets fail to be efficient. Special attention is paid to the analysis of strategic behavior and markets with public goods and externalities.

subject: ECON

subject: ECON
title: The Japanese Economy
description: Analysis of Japan's growth past and present. Does Japan's economy look different in terms of its international trade structure, industrial structure, labor market, savings patterns, government policies, etc.? Does it matter?

subject: ECON

description: and sustainable tourism development.

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

Considers the history of views on work, poverty, the market and government, and the relationship of those doctrines to society, philosophy, and public policy. Frequency of Course (when offered): Alt years ECON 361 - Seminar: Women and International Development - Credits: 3
prerequisites: ECON 120, ECON 130, or ECON 131; or consent.

title: Sustainable Development

Topics include: curse of paradise, global warming, energy use, health, poverty, population, water resources, traffic congestion, biodiversity, pollution controls.
prerequisites: ECON 120 or ECON 130 or ECON 131, or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

prerequisites: a 100 level economics course or any women's studies course; or consent.

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

subject: ECON
title: Monetary Theory and Policy

subject: ECON

Microfoundations and critical analysis of monetary and macroeconomic theory and policy. Topics include the causes and consequences of inflation, optimal monetary policy and international monetary systems, bank risk and insurance, and national debt and taxation.

Topics on production, consumption, labor market, asset market, economic growth, and business cycles are discussed with emphasis on real-economy applications and policy analysis. grade_option: A-F only. ECON 604 - Microeconomics for Business and Policy Analysis - Credits: 3 Theory of the consumer, firm, and market. Perfect competition and efficiency. Market power and firm strategy. Market efficiencies. Role of the government.

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

subject: ECON
title: Microeconomic Theory II
description: General equilibrium analysis: production, consumption and Walrasian equilibria; Pareto efficiency, fundamental theorems of welfare economics; externalities; public goods; game theory; information theory.

subject: ECON

description: Game theory and strategic behavior. Economics of information and incentives principal-agent theory. Economic design. Applications include: theory of contracts; incentive compatible mechanism for provision of public goods; auction theory.

Externalities, public goods, non-convexities, regulation; cost-benefit analysis, general equilibrium, shadow-pricing; rent-seeking, corruption; optimal taxation, incidence, excess burden; dynamic public finance, national debt, social security.

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

subject: ECON

description: Supervised post-internship I experience in clinical rehabilitation or school counseling at an approved site, including a weekly class meeting. Provides practical application of formal academic training. (C) community service; (E) elementary; (H) secondary; (R) rehabilitation; (U) college.
repeatable: Each alpha is repeatable three times.

subject: EDEP
title: Seminar in School Counseling
description: In-depth study of professional concerns in school counseling.

subject: EDEP
title: Creative Learning Strategies for Adults
description: Analysis of psychology of adult learner; forces that affect learning in dynamics of individual, group, and organizational behavior; concept of lifelong learning visà-vis development of creative strategies that assist maturing, self-directed persons to develop their potentialities.

subject: EDEP
title: (Alpha) Seminar in Educational Psychology
description: Current issues and problems in the context of education: (B) general; (C) learning; (D) measurement; (E) statistics; (F) psycho-social development; (G) educational evaluation; (H) research methodology.
repeatable: Each alpha is repeatable three times.

subject: EDEP
title: Internship II: rehabilitation
description: Supervised post-internship I experience in clinical rehabilitation or school counseling at an approved site, including a weekly class meeting. Provides practical application of formal academic training. (C) community service; (E) elementary; (H) secondary; (R) rehabilitation; (U) college. EDEP 768B - Seminar in Educational Psychology: general - Credits: 3 Current issues and problems in the context of education: (B) general; (C) learning; (D) measurement; (E) statistics; (F) psycho-social development; (G) educational evaluation; (H) research methodology. EDEP 768C - Seminar in Educational Psychology: learning - Credits: 3 Current issues and problems in the context of education: (B) general; (C) learning; (D) measurement; (E) statistics; (F) psycho-social development; (G) educational evaluation; (H) research methodology. EDEP 768H - Seminar in Educational Psychology: research methodology - Credits: 3 Current issues and problems in the context of education: (B) general; (C) learning; (D) measurement; (E) statistics; (F) psycho-social development; (G) educational evaluation; (H) research methodology.
repeatable: 9 times. Seminar may be repeated for credit as topics vary.

subject: EDEP
title: Seminar in Rehabilitation Counseling
description: In-depth study of professional concerns in rehabilitation counseling.

subject: EDEP
title: Dissertation Research
description: Research for doctoral dissertation. Electrical and Computer Engineering Preference in registration is given to declared engineering majors. Enrollment in ECE courses requires a grade of C or better in all prerequisite courses, unless otherwise specified.

subject: ECE
title: Electrical Engineering Skills
description: Electrical engineering subjects in a skill acquisition context at the freshman level. Learning, creative problem solving, brainstorming, technical information assimilation, and presentation skills development.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Engineering Computation
description: Engineering problem solving using MATLAB. Basic programming concepts include input/output, branching, looping, functions, file input/output, and data structures such as arrays and structures. Matrix operations for solving linear equations. Engineering computations and visualization.
prerequisites: MATH 241 (or concurrent) or MATH 251A (or concurrent) or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Programming for Engineers
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Introduction to computer programming and modern computing environments with an emphasis on algorithm and program design, implementation, and debugging. Includes a hands-on laboratory to develop and practice programming skills.
prerequisites: MATH 241 (or concurrent) or MATH 251A (or concurrent) or consent.
major_restrictions: BENG, CE, CENG, EE, ME, PREN, CNST, and ENGS majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Freshman Project
description: Freshman level individual or team project under ECE faculty direction and guidance. This project provides early student entry into ECE hands-on project activity providing practical skills, ECE subject exposure and experience. Second semester freshman standing required.
major_restrictions: CENG, EE, and PREN majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Object Oriented Programming
description: Second programming for computer engineers. System programming language, such as C. Object-oriented programming paradigm, definition and use of classes, fundamentals of object-oriented design, such as C++. Common data structures. Common searching and sorting algorithms.
major_restrictions: CEE, CENG, EE, ME, PREN majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Basic Circuit Analysis I
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Linear passive circuits, time domain analysis, transient and steady-state responses, phasors, impedance and admittance; power and energy, frequency responses, resonance.
prerequisites: MATH 243 (or concurrent) or MATH 253A (or concurrent), and PHYS 272 (or concurrent); or consent.
major_restrictions: BENG, CE, CENG, EE, ME, PREN, and ENGS majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Basic Circuit Analysis II
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Laplace transforms and their application to circuits, Fourier transforms and their applications to circuits, frequency selective circuits, introduction to and design of active filters, convolution, and state space analysis of circuits.
prerequisites: ECE 211, and MATH 244 (or concurrent) or MATH 253A (or concurrent); or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Digital Design
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Introduction to the design of digital systems with an emphasis on design methods and the implementation and use of fundamental digital components.
prerequisites: ECE 160 or ECE 110 or ICS 111 or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Sophomore Project
description: Sophomore level individual or team project under ECE faculty direction and guidance. The project provides design experience and develops practical skills.
major_restrictions: CENG, EE, and PREN majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Signal and Systems Analysis
description: Discrete-time and continuous time signals and systems, linear systems, convolution, Fourier series, Fourier transform, sampling.
prerequisites: ECE 213 and either MATH 244 or MATH 253A; or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Microelectronic Circuits I
description: Semiconductor structures, operating principles and characteristics of diodes and amplifying devices. Their application as circuit elements in building basic digital, analog, and integrated circuit subsystems.

subject: ECE
title: Microelectronic Circuits I Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Experiments on linear and logic properties of diodes and transistor networks.

subject: ECE
title: Physical Electronics
description: Review of quantum mechanics fundamentals, H-atom, and chemical bonding. Introduction to band structure models and materials. Semiconductor doping, charge carrier statistics and charge transport, including ambipolar transport. Metal-semiconductor and PN junctions.
prerequisites: MATH 243 or MATH 253A, and PHYS 274; or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Microelectronic Circuits II
description: Principles and design of linear electronic circuits including differential, operational, feedback, and tuned amplifiers; integrated circuits, current mirrors, signal generators, filters, and stability.

subject: ECE
title: Microcircuit Fabrication Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Hands-on laboratory where students make various electronic and electromechanical micro-devices using IC technology. Devices are also tested and analyzed.

subject: ECE
title: Probability and Statistics
description: Probability, statistics, random variables, distributions, densities, expectations, limit theorems, and applications to electrical engineering.
prerequisites: ECE 315 (or concurrent) and either MATH 244 or MATH 253A; or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Communication Systems
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Laboratory for ECE 326, experiments on linear and analog electronics. Includes an emphasis on writing laboratory reports. (1 3-hr Lab) Experiments illustrating the basic principles of communication systems. Includes an emphasis on instruction in writing laboratory reports.

subject: ECE
title: Theory and Design of IC Devices
description: Band structure models and carrier transport physics review. Theory and design of semiconductor IC devices: Schottky diodes, bipolar devices (PN junction diodes, BJTs), FETs (MOSFETs, JFETs, and MESFETs).
prerequisites: ECE 324 and either MATH 243 or MATH 253A; or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Microcircuit Fabrication
description: Technology principles, materials, and methods for the design and fabrication of semiconductor devices, integrated circuits, and microelectromechanical

subject: ECE
title: Networking I
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Covers 4 semesters from the Cisco Networking Academy plus supplementary material; hands-on experience with routers and switches; prepares students for the CCNA. Topics include TCP/IP, LANs, WANs, routing protocols, network security; PPP; ISDN, frame relay.

subject: ECE
title: Linear Algebra and Machine Learning
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Mathematical and algorithmic fundamentals of linear algebra and their applications and illustrations to machine learning. Lab introduces programming with data and uses machine learning libraries for an introduction to commonly used technologies.
major_restrictions: MATH, CENG, EE, CE, ME, ICS majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Linear Feedback-Control Systems
description: Analysis/design of feedback systems. Compensator design via root locus and Bode analysis. Routh/Nyquist stability. State space representation and introduction to MIMO formulation. Controllability/observability. Application to physical dynamic systems such as industrial robots.

subject: ECE
title: Linear Feedback-Control Systems Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Provides experience in applying theoretical tools to analyze linear systems. Extensive use is made of computer-aided analysis and design packages study system performance.

subject: ECE
title: Digital Systems and Computer Design
description: Design methodology, processor design, control design, memory organization, system organization.

subject: ECE
title: Digital Systems and Computer Design Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Laboratory for ECE 361, experiments on digital systems and interfacing. Includes an emphasis on instruction in writing laboratory reports.

subject: ECE
title: Discrete Math for Engineers
description: properties of relations, methods of proofs, recursion, counting, probability, trees, graphs, analysis of algorithms, finite state autonoma.

subject: ECE
title: CMOS VLSI Design
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Introduction to the design of very large scale integrated (VLSI) systems and use of CAD tools and design languages. Lab includes hands-on use of CAD tools and experiments with field programmable logic devices.

subject: ECE
title: Computer Data Structures and Algorithms
description: Design and analysis of data structures and algorithms, including correctness and performance. Topics include time complexity, hash tables, sorting, search trees, selfbalancing trees, greedy algorithms, dynamic programming, and graph algorithms.

subject: ECE
title: Computational Media Systems
description: Intermediate object-oriented programming within the context of interactive media systems and video game development. Topics: classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, abstract classes, interfaces, eventdriven programming, vectors, geometric primitives, game mechanics, and relevant design patterns.
prerequisites: ECE 160 or ICS 111 or instructor approval.
major_restrictions: EE, CENG, ICS, CM, CSCI, THEA, DNCE, THDN majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Engineering Electromagnetics I
description: Transient and steady-state waves on transmission lines. Plane wave solutions of Maxwell's equations. Application of Maxwell's equations under static and time-varying conditions.

subject: ECE
title: Engineering Electromagnetics II
description: Solution of Maxwell's equations under various boundary conditions. Introduction to radiation, guided waves, and principles of optics.
prerequisites: ECE 371 and PHYS 274 (or concurrent); or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Engineering Electromagnetics Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Experiments illustrating the basic principles of electromagnetics and optics.
prerequisites: ECE 371 and PHYS 274 (or concurrent), or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Junior Project
description: Junior level individual or team project under EE faculty direction and guidance. The project provides design experience and develops practical skills. It may be a continuation of ECE 296 or a new project.
prerequisites: ECE 361 or ICS 312 or ICS 331 or instructor consent.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year
cross_listed: ENGR 396 ECE 406 - Introduction to Computer and Network Security - Credits: 3 Review basic network mechanisms, introduce basic cryptography concepts, and study algorithms and protocols used in computer and network security. Discuss practical security mechanisms.

subject: ECE
title: Digital Signal Processing
description: (3 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Discrete-time signals and systems, sampling, Z-transform, transform, transform analysis of linear time-invariant systems, filter design, discrete Fourier transform, and computation of discrete Fourier transform.
prerequisites: ECE 315 and ECE 342 (or concurrent), or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Digital Image Processing
description: Digital image representation, intensity transformations, spatial filtering, filtering in the frequency domain, image restoration, color spaces and transformations, the fast wavelet transform, image compression.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Optimization
description: Application of linear, nonlinear and integer optimization models and algorithms to communications, control, signal processing, computer networking, financial engineering, manufacturing, production and distribution systems.
prerequisites: (ECE 160, ECE 211, and BE 350 or MATH 302 or MATH 307 or ECE 326) with a minimum grade of C; or consent.
major_restrictions: CE, CENG, EE, ME, or CBA majors only. ECE 422 - Sensors and Instrumentation for Biological Systems - Credits: 3 Design course focused on fundamentals of electronic interfacing, control and automation, including biological processes. Topics include sensor physics, basic instrumentation, digital communication, and programming of microcontrollers and other portable computer systems.

subject: ECE
title: Instrumentation Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Laboratory for ECE 422.

subject: ECE
title: Computer-Aided Analysis and Design
description: Algorithms and techniques used in computer-aided analysis and design of electronic circuits. Circuit simulation with interactive computers.

subject: ECE
title: Electronic Instrumentation II
description: Instrumentation systems and circuits for measurement, control, signal processing, transmission, and detection. Noise and interference, ADC/DAC, modulation demodulation, high-frequency and high-speed techniques, IC applications.

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Si IC and Solid State Devices
description: State of the art Si-based devices including advanced bipolar and MOS devices, heterojunction devices, new device trends. Topics from the most current literature included.
prerequisites: ECE 327 and either MATH 243 or MATH 253A, or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Computer-Aided Circuit Design
description: Application of the computer to the analysis, design, simulation, and construction of analog and digital circuits.

subject: ECE
title: Renewable Energy
description: Fundamentals of power, electric power grid and conventional electricity generation. Wind and solar power systems. Photovoltaic materials and systems. Distributed generation and energy storage.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ECE
title: Digital Communications
description: Baseband transmission, intersymbol interference and pulse shaping, partial response signaling, equalization, bandpass modulation and demodulation, channel coding, synchronization, multiplexing and multiple access, spread spectrum techniques.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Machine Learning
description: Foundation for algorithms, practice, and theory behind common machine-learning applications. Includes projects, statistical programming, and an introduction to the unique challenges of high-dimensional problems.
major_restrictions: EE, CENG, CEE, ME, MATH, ICS majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Information Theory and Coding
description: Models of communication systems. Channel noise, measurement, and coding of information. Intrinsic limits of performance of communication systems.

subject: ECE
title: Electric Power Systems
description: Design/operation of "the grid." History of electric power systems, three-phrase power, real and reactive power, transformers, transmission, distribution, circuit analysis, protection, load flow, load frequency control, optimal power flow, and renewable energy integration.

subject: ECE
title: Computer Communication Networks
description: ISO Reference Model. Physical Layer, Data Link Layer, Network Layer and Transport Layer protocols. Wired and wireless local-area networks. Structure and operation of the Internet including routing, congestion control and flow control.
prerequisites: ECE 315 and one of ECE 342, or MATH 371 or MATH 471; or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Digital Control Systems
description: Sampling/reconstruction, Z-transform, DT transfer function. Reachability/observability. State and output feedback, observer design, input-output models, diophantine equations. Implementation procedures.

subject: ECE
title: Modern Control Theory
description: Analysis and synthesis of nonlinear control systems by means of Lagrange's equation, state space techniques, maximum principle. Lyapunov's theorems, the phase plane, and Z-transform techniques. Optimization and adaptation by means of gradient methods, calculus of variations, dynamic programming.

subject: ECE
title: Design of Intelligent Robots
description: Study of the design principles of computer-controlled, intelligent robots such as roving vehicles, hand-eye systems.

subject: ECE
title: Computer Architecture
description: Structure of stored program machines, data flow machines, pipelining, fault-tolerant computing, instruction set design, effects of compilation on architecture, RISC vs. CISC architecture, uses of parallelism.

subject: ECE
title: Object-oriented Software Engineering
description: Introduction to advanced techniques for designing, implementing, and testing computer software with a particular focus on using object-oriented design, analysis, and programming to produce high-quality computer programs that solve non-trivial problems.

subject: ECE
title: Introduction to Operating Systems
description: Emphasis on written communication through written assignments on relevant topics.
prerequisites: ECE 361 (or concurrent) and ECE 367 or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Wireless Data Networks
description: Mobile agent's platforms and systems, mobile agentbased service implementation, middleware, and configuration, wireless local area networks, wireless protocols, network architecture supporting wireless applications, routing protocols in mobile and wireless networks, handoff in mobile and wireless networks.

subject: ECE
title: Physical Optics
description: Fundamentals of classical physical optics emphasizing linear systems theory, including optical fields in matter, polarization phenomena, temporal coherence, interference and diffraction (Fourier optics). Specialized applications include Gaussian beams, laser resonators, pulse propagation, and nonlinear optics.
gen_ed: DP ECE 471 - Computational Techniques in Electromagnetics - Credits: 3 Introduction to computational methods used to simulate/solve engineering design problems focusing on electromagnetics. Finite difference, method of moments, and finite elements methods will be described; students will write computer programs in each.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only
major_restrictions: BE, CENG, EE, ENGR majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Microwave Engineering
description: Passive and active microwave devices and circuits for RF and wireless applications. Scattering parameters, signalflow graphs, and computer-aided design.

subject: ECE
title: Antennas
description: Electromagnetic wave propagation in free space and ionized media. Geomagnetic and solar effects on the ionosphere. Absorption and dispersion. Antenna arrays, apertures, horns, impedance. Design of antenna systems.

subject: ECE
title: Bioelectric Phenomena
description: Study of electrical phenomena in living systems. Mechanisms underlying bioelectric activity. Membrane and transepithelial potentials, skin impedance, electrocardiography, neuroelectric signals, diagnostic considerations, laboratory demonstrations.

subject: ECE
title: Optical Communications
description: Principles and applications of optical fibers and waveguides. Fundamentals of optical communication systems (optical links, high-speed systems, wavelengthdivision-multiplexing networks, and network elements) and optical components (guided-wave circuits, lasers, detectors, and optical amplifiers). System and network integration issues.

subject: ECE
title: Biomedical Instrumentation
description: ECE 491 - (Alpha) Special Topics in Electrical Engineering - Credits: 3 ECE 477 - Fundamentals of Radar, Sonar, and Navigation Systems - Credits: 3 Discussion of basic radar detection and position- and velocity-measurement principles. Applications to various types of radar and sonar systems. Modern navigation aids. (2 Lec, 1 3-hr Lab) Principles, applications, and design of biomedical instrumentation. Transducers, IC and microcomputer applications, patient safety. Content will reflect special interests of visiting/permanent faculty; to be oriented toward juniors and seniors. (B) artificial intelligence; (C) circuits; (D) communications; (E) computer hardware; (F) computer software; (G) computer vision; (H) control; (I) devices; (J) fields; (K) power. ECE 491C - Special Topics in Electrical Engineering: circuits - Credits: 3
prerequisites: ECE 213 and either MATH 244 or MATH 253A.
gen_ed: DP ECE 480 - Introduction to Biomedical and Clinical Engineering - Credits: 3 Application of engineering principles and technology to biological and medical problems. Introduction to human anatomy, physiology, medical terminology, clinical measurements. Systems modeling, physiological control systems, computer applications, health-related problems.

subject: ECE
title: Biomedical Engineering Lab
description: (1 3-hr Lab) Measurement of bioelectrical signals, computer and electronic simulation of biological systems, design and evaluation of electronic circuits for biomedical measurements, evaluation of instruments for patient safety. subject: ECE
title: Provisional Topics
description: Upper division course with subject matter to be announced.

subject: ECE
title: Ethics in Electrical Engineering
description: Equip electrical engineers with the necessary background for ethical reasoning, as it pertains to technology, society, workplace issues, and the environment.
grade_option: A-F only. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year

subject: ECE
title: Capstone Design Project
description: Significant project integrating multiple aspects of the design process: reliability, safety, economics, environment, social, ethics, teamwork. Includes an emphasis on instruction and feedback in oral and written communication.

subject: ECE
title: Directed Reading
description: Investigation of advanced engineering problems.

subject: ECE
title: Graph Theory and Its Applications
description: Graphs and subgraphs, trees and treelike graphs, planar graphs, connectivity and edge-connectivity, applications.

subject: ECE
title: Algorithm I
description: ECE 605 - Advanced Large-Scale Artificial Intelligence Systems - Credits: 3 Explains the technical challenges of developing, deploying, and using large language models. Equips students with the skills to conduct AI research in academia or industry, emphasizing advanced techniques, rigorous evaluation, and ethical considerations in applications.
major_restrictions: Engineering, ICS, math, business, and economics majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Intelligent Autonomous Agents
description: Theory, methods and practical applications of autonomous agent systems, including common applications of both software and hardware (robotic) agents. In-depth practical experience with autonomous agents through programming assignments and projects.
prerequisites: ECE 467 or ICS 313 (or equivalent), graduate standing; or consent. Frequency of Course (when offered): Once a year

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Network Algorithms
description: Network algorithms, protocols, and packet switching systems for the internet including TCP/IP, routing algorithms, transmission scheduling, link management, buffer management, and simple network management.

subject: ECE
title: Optical Networks
description: Propagation of signals in fibers, components, modulation and demodulation, transmission system engineering, network systems and architectures, network design, control and management and packet switching.
prerequisites: ECE 342, ECE 367, and ECE 371; or consent.

subject: ECE
title: Artificial Intelligence
description: Basic security theory, current practices, and emerging research issues. First covering the fundamentals of computer and network security, then will work on research projects on computer and network security. LISP for machine intelligence applications, or related constraint object and logic-oriented languages.

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Digital Signal Processing
description: An advanced course in digital processing. Topics include fast DFT algorithms, multirate systems and filter banks, power spectrum estimation, linear prediction, optimum linear filters, and adaptive filtering.
grade_option: A-F only. Open to nonmajors for CR/NC only.

subject: ECE
title: Digital Image Processing
description: Human visual perception, image formation, sampling and quantization, enhancement and restoration, color image processing, wavelets and multiresolution representations, image and video compression.

subject: ECE
title: Linear and Convex Optimization
description: Algorithms for linear, nonlinear, and convex optimization. Emphasis is on methodology and the underlying mathematical structures. Topics include simplex method, network flow methods, optimality conditions, duality, Newton's method and interior point methods.
prerequisites: ECE 342 or MATH 371 or MATH 471, or consent.
major_restrictions: ECE, ME, MIS and MATH majors only. ECE 618 - Dynamic Programming and Stochastic Control - Credits: 3 Sequential decision-making via dynamic programming. Optimal control of stochastic dynamic systems. Applications in linear-quadratic control, inventory control, resource allocation, scheduling, and control of queues. Rollout and other suboptimal methods. Value and policy iteration.

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Electronic Circuits
description: Electronic circuits for precision measurement, computation, and signal processing. Low noise and interference reduction techniques. High-frequency and high-speed techniques. Micro-processor and biomedical applications.

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Solid-State Devices
description: of current importance identified from the current literature.

subject: ECE
title: Optical Electronics I
description: Fundamentals of optical radiation, including stimulated and spontaneous processes. Optical electronics including optical resonators, lasers, optical detectors, light-guiding, and applications.

subject: ECE
title: Optical Electronics II
description: Electro-optics, noise detection, light and sound dielectric waveguide phenomena, lasers, optics, phase conjugation.

subject: ECE
title: Microsensors and Microactuators I
description: Technology methods and physical principles of microsensors and microactuators. Vacuum technology, thin film deposition and characterization techniques, solid mechanics, micromachining, acoustics, piezoelectricity and principles of current microtransducers.
prerequisites: ECE 327 or consent. ECE 626 - Rapid Prototyping of Electrophysical Devices - Credits: 3 Hands on experience in designing, fabricating, testing, and iterating according to rapid-prototyping principles. Students learn state-of-the-art equipment for making their designs, software for simulating designs, and working in design teams. Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Topics in Physical Electronics
description: Recent developments in phenomena and devices of physical electronics.

subject: ECE
title: Analysis and Design of Integrated Circuits
description: Fabrication constraints and design guidelines for integrated circuits. Nonlinear model of integrated circuit transistor. Design and analysis of integrated logic circuits and linear circuits.
prerequisites: ECE 435 Frequency of Course (when offered): Spring only

subject: ECE
title: Applied Random Processes
description: Random variables, multivariate distributions, random sequences, stochastic convergence, stationary and nonstationary processes, spectral analysis, KarhunenLoeve expansion, Markov processes, mean square estimation, Kalman filters.

subject: ECE
title: Queueing Theory
description: Poisson, Markov, and renewal processes, M/G/1 queue, G/M/1 queue, queueing networks, simulation, and performance evaluation of computer systems and communication networks.

subject: ECE
title: Detection and Estimation Theory
description: Fundamentals of signal detection and estimation theory. Hypothesis testing, parametric and nonparametric detection, sequential detection, parametric estimation, linear estimation, robust detection and estimation, and applications to communication systems.

subject: ECE
title: Communication System Performance
description: estimation, modulation, intersymbol interference, equalization adaptive filtering, sequence detection, synchronization, fading multipath channels, spread spectrum.

subject: ECE
title: Computer Communication Networks
description: Fundamentals of computer communication networks including modeling, performance evaluation, routing, flow control, local area networks, distributed algorithms, and optimization algorithms.

subject: ECE
title: Machine Learning
description: Learning theory, pattern recognition and regression; gradient based algorithms and least square algorithms; Kernel methods; Bayesian learning algorithms; ensemble learning and boosting; principal component analysis; independent component analysis, and clustering; reinforcement learning and approximate dynamic programming.
major_restrictions: ECE, ME, ICS, MATH majors only.

subject: ECE
title: Advanced Information Theory
description: Measure of information, coding for discrete sources, discrete memoryless channels and capacity, the noisy channel coding theorem, source coding with fidelity criterion, rate-distortion theory, multiuser channels.

subject: ECE
title: Source Coding
description: Theory and applications of source coding, rate-distortion theory, companding, lattice coding, tree coding, trellis coding, entropy-constrained coding, asymptotic theory, predictive and differential encoding, combined source/channel coding, vector quantization.

subject: ECE
title: Error-Control Coding I
description: Linear block codes, soft and hard decision decodings, correction of random errors, cyclic codes, BCH codes, Reed-Solomon codes, majority logic decodable codes, burst-error correcting codes, concatenated codes.

subject: ECE
title: Error-Control Coding II
description: communication, distributed systems, device drivers. Software development and maintenance, integration of software packages. Projects reflecting special interests of faculty.

subject: ECE
title: Linear System Theory
description: Telecommunication-network architecture; switching, broadcast, and wireless networks; protocols, interfaces, routing, flow- and congestion-control techniques; intelligent network architecture; service creation capabilities; multimedia, voice, data, and video networks and services.

subject: ECE
title: Nonlinear Control Systems
description: Digital simulations, phase-plane analysis, limit cycles and amplitude bounds, Lyapunov's theorem, circle criterion of stability, lure systems, Popov's stability theorem.

subject: ECE
title: Wireless and Mobile Security
description: State space theory of linear systems, controllability, observability, stability, irreducible realizations.

subject: ECE
